method: Chirikov resonance overlap test (peak-vs-peak reformulation). Extracted spectral peaks from LA2004 eccentricity and obliquity (Welch PSD, full 51 Myr, period range 14.0-500.0 kyr) with FWHM widths. Computed local Chirikov K = Σ(widths)/Σ(separations) in ±15.0% around each peak. Tagged peaks as L1-aligned (within 3% of any L1 integer) vs non-L1. Compared K distributions.
verdict: NULL BUT INFORMATIVE — ALL spectral peaks are KAM-stable: L1-aligned 96% stable, non-L1 100% stable, Mann-Whitney p=0.667. This says KAM theory is the GENERAL property of LA2004's spectrum (which is what we'd expect for a quasi-periodic Hamiltonian solution), but it does NOT single out L1 from the broader set of stable peaks. L1 captures 23/33=70% of the stable spectral peaks, but the remaining 10 non-L1 peaks are ALSO KAM-stable. The mechanism that singles out L1 must be more specific than KAM stability — likely the 8H quantization itself (periodicity in action-angle space) rather than resonance-overlap protection.
